line_1: The Moorish slaves aloft their cymbals swung,
line_2: With hoarse acclaim, the gong and trumpet rung,

line_1: And, fuming high, the silver censer swung;
line_2: At Easter-tide, when the high mass was sung,

line_1: Marched, whilst the plumed Moors their cymbals swung.
line_2: The arbalasters next, with cross-bows slung,

line_1: Who, as the ringing harp he swung,
line_2: Rais'd his sweet voice and rapid tongue
